column1: You can just see a thin thread of light under the door, so there is probably someone inside.
column2: 你隱約看到門下傳來一絲光線，所以可能裡面有人。

column1: The Guild Hall building appears to be dark and closed up.
column2: 冒險者公會的建築看起來很暗，而且關門了。

column1: You enter the Adventurers' Guild Hall.
column2: 你進入了冒險者公會大廳。

column1: The door appears to be locked.
column2: 門看起來被鎖上了。

column1: Your dirk doesn't work on the door.
column2: 你的短匕首在這扇門上沒有用。

column1: That key doesn't fit this lock.
column2: 那把鑰匙不適合這個鎖。

column1: Judging from what the sign says, the building at the end of the street is the Guild Hall.
column2: 根據標誌上的內容判斷，街道盡頭的建築就是冒險者公會。

column1: This looks like a nice, neat little house.  There is a grandmotherly Little Old Lady rocking on the front porch.
column2: 這看起來是一棟漂亮、整潔的小房子。有一位溫暖如祖母般的老奶奶在前廊搖椅上搖晃。

column1: The house looks closed up.  There is no sign of the Little Old Lady.
column2: 房子看起來關門了。看不到那位老奶奶。

column1: The door appears to be locked, and there is no answer to your knock.  The occupant is out (right in front of you, in fact).
column2: 門看起來被鎖上了，敲門也沒人應。住戶不在（其實就在你正前方）。

column1: No one seems to be home, and the door is securely locked.
column2: 似乎沒人在家，門也鎖得很牢。

column1: This isn't the best place to practice for your woodcarving merit badge.
column2: 這裡不是練習木雕獎章的最好地方。

column1: The key doesn't fit.
column2: 鑰匙不適合。

column1: She's the only sane person here.  At least she's not off her rocker.
column2: 她是這裡唯一清醒的人。至少她還沒失心瘋。

column1: The little old lady is sleeping so soundly that no amount of shaking will awaken her.
column2: 這位老奶奶睡得很沉，再怎麼搖也叫不醒她。

column1: She's really sleeping soundly and doesn't notice your presence.
column2: 她睡得真沉，沒注意到你的存在。

column1: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ!
column2: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼！」

column1: Grmpf..snrt..hmphspft!
column2: 「唔嚯…唔…唔普！」

column1: The eye over the Magic Shop door seems to be watching you.
column2: 魔法店門上的眼睛似乎在盯著你看。

column1: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.
column2: 不過，這裡不是睡覺的好地方。你全身酸痛，錢包也變輕了不少。

column1: Picking locks looked a lot easier in the instruction book.
column2: 開鎖在教科書裡看起來容易多了。

column1: The door isn't locked.  Just turn around.
column2: 門沒鎖。轉身就行了。

column1: The owners seem to have been made nervous by the recent rash of house burglaries.  The door is barred from the inside.  But you do get some valuable lock-picking practice.
column2: 業主似乎被最近頻繁的入室盜竊嚇得神經兮兮。門從裡面被擋住了。但你確實獲得了寶貴的開鎖練習。

column1: You hear a "Snick!".  The lock is open!
column2: 你聽到了「卡！」一聲。鎖打開了！

column1: You have come to the end of the main street.  The town wall is to the south. You have an eerie feeling that someone is watching you.
column2: 你來到了主街的盡頭。城牆在南邊。你有種被誰盯著的怪異感覺。

column1: There's no getting around it... a shop with its eye on you makes you nervous.
column2: 無可奈何…一家眼睛盯著你的店鋪讓你神經緊張。

column1: Not the sturdiest lock you've ever seen.
column2: 不是你見過最堅固的鎖。

column1: This is certainly a weird one!  You've never seen anything quite like it.
column2: 這肯定很詭異！你從未見過這樣的東西。

column1: The plaque reads: "Antwerp -- slain by Two Guys From Andromeda".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「安特衛普——被仙女座來的兩個傢伙殺死」。

column1: A past adventurer's armor suit.  You wonder why he doesn't need it any more. (Then you see the Cheetaur claw marks.)
column2: 一套過去冒險者的盔甲。你想知道他為什麼不再需要它。（然後你看到了獵豹人的爪痕。）

column1: The armor won't fit you -- they made them smaller in those days.
column2: 盔甲不適合你——他們那時做得比較小。

column1: On the table is an old but ornate leather-bound book.  There is also a quill pen in the ink bottle.
column2: 桌上有一本舊但華麗的皮革精裝書。墨瓶裡還有一支鵝毛筆。

column1: The writing on this page is very recent.  It says "I, %s, have come to Spielburg to become a hero."
column2: 這頁上的字跡很新。它寫著「我，%s，來到史畢柏格成為英雄。」

column1: This entry was made several years ago. It says: 'Baronet Barnard von Spielburg killed a Troll near the Flying Falls on this 23rd day of Octember'.
column2: 這項記錄是幾年前寫的。它寫著：「子爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格在十月二十三日在飛瀑附近殺死了一隻巨魔」。

column1: The other pages record the exploits of the great adventurers of Spielburg's past.  The names Wolfgang Abenteuer and Baron Stefan von Spielburg appear most often.
column2: 其他頁面記錄了史畢柏格過去偉大冒險者的事蹟。沃夫岡·阿本特爾和史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的名字最常出現。

column1: The remaining pages are blank, awaiting further tales of adventure.
column2: 剩下的頁面是空白的，等待更多冒險故事。

column1: You sign your name into the Adventurer's Log Book with a flourish.
column2: 你在冒險者日誌中簽下你的名字，做得很瀟灑。

column1: But you've already done that!
column2: 但你已經做過了！

column1: The fine crystal chandelier lights the room.
column2: 精緻的水晶吊燈照亮了房間。

column1: You can't reach it.
column2: 你摸不著。

column1: The head is like a panther's, but with a strong human-like quality.  It is still rather frightening.
column2: 這頭像豹子的頭，但帶著強烈的人類特質。看起來還是很嚇人。

column1: The plaque reads: "Cheetaur -- slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「獵豹人——被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

column1: The table holds a pen, an inkwell, and the Adventurer's Guild Registration and Log Book.
column2: 桌上放著一支筆、墨瓶和冒險者公會登記冊及日誌。

column1: The table is formed from fine fir wood.  There's nothing you need here.
column2: 桌子是用優質冷杉木製成的。這裡沒有你需要的東西。

column1: Even in death, this monster remains awesome.
column2: 即使死了，這隻怪物仍然令人敬畏。

column1: The plaque reads: "Dragon -- slain by Baron Stefan von Spielburg".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「龍——被史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵殺死」。

column1: The fire blazing cheerily in the fireplace helps warm the Guild Master's old bones.
column2: 壁爐裡歡快燃燒的火焰幫助溫暖了公會主人的老骨頭。

column1: You feel like you're frying your fingers.  Forget it.
column2: 你覺得你的手指要烤焦了。算了。

column1: This crossbreed of eagle and lion could have torn a man apart when it was alive.
column2: 這隻鷲獅雜交獸活著的時候可能能撕裂一個人。

column1: The plaque reads: "Gryphon -- slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「獅鷲——被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

column1: The plaque under the Moose says: "Courtesy of the Sierra Online Prop Dept."
column2: 駝鹿下的銘牌寫著：「蒙《Sierra Online》道具部提供」。

column1: The tapestry is so rotten, it would probably fall apart at the slightest touch.
column2: 掛毯爛得很厲害，稍微一碰可能就會散開。

column1: The tapestry feels very old and very fragile.
column2: 掛毯摸起來非常古老，也非常脆弱。

column1: This Adventurer's Guild Hall reminds you of the one in your home town.  The traditional Moose head and other stuffed monsters (Saurus, Troll, Gryphon, Dragon, Cheetaur, and the terrible Antwerp) adorn the walls.
column2: 這個冒險者公會大廳讓你想起你家鄉的那個。傳統的駝鹿頭和其他標本怪物（索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲、龍、獵豹人和可怕的安特衛普）裝飾著牆壁。

column1: You see a registration book on the table, and the bulletin board full of job listings.  The man seated near the window must be the Guild Master.  He is snoring.
column2: 你看到桌上有本登記冊，公告板上滿是工作清單。坐在窗邊的男人一定是公會主人。他在打呼。

column1: There is nothing here that you need, and the Guild Master may be more aware than he seems.
column2: 這裡沒有你需要的東西，公會主人可能比看起來更清醒。

column1: The Guild Hall would be barren if you took the trophies.  Besides, they're out of reach.
column2: 如果你拿走獎杯，公會大廳就會空蕩蕩的。而且，它們也夠不著。

column1: You never saw a purple Saurus before you came to Spielburg, but it looks like a really stupid monster.
column2: 來史畢柏格之前，你從未見過紫色的索魯斯，但它看起來像一隻非常愚蠢的怪物。

column1: The plaque reads: "Saurus slain by Hans Halfwitten".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「索魯斯被漢斯·哈夫威騰殺死」。

column1: The view from here isn't exactly clear.
column2: 從這裡看的景色不太清楚。

column1: It looks like it must have been a particularly nasty Troll.  You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark forest.
column2: 看起來這一定是隻特別凶惡的巨魔。你不會想在暗森林裡遇見他。

column1: The plaque reads: "Troll slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
column2: 銘牌上寫著：「巨魔被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

column1: On the walls hang dusty, moth-eaten monster heads.  You recognize the purple Saurus, the Troll, the Gryphon, and the Dragon.
column2: 牆上掛著佈滿灰塵、被蟲蛀的怪物頭顱。你認出了紫色的索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲和龍。

column1: The window looks out over the countryside.
column2: 窗戶俯瞰鄉村。

column1: The window doesn't open.
column2: 窗戶打不開。

column1: Welcome back!  It is good to see you again.  So many adventurers never return.  Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together.
column2: 「歡迎回來！很高興再看到你。那麼多冒險者都再也回不來了。有一天，你和我會一起去冒險。」

column1: Hrrmph!  Ahem!  Yes, as I was saying...  Where were we?
column2: 「呃唔！咳！是的，我剛才說到…我們說到哪裡了？」

column1: Oh, what a glorious day it was!  I...  Oh, hello there!
column2: 「噢，那是多麼光榮的一天啊！我…噢，你好！」

column1: You fiendish witch!  I will have you...  Oh, it's you, my friend!
column2: 「你這邪惡的女巫！我會…噢，是你啊，我的朋友！」

column1: Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon... What?  What?  You're still here, my fellow adventurer?
column2: 「噢，巴納德，最勇敢的勇者，你不能這麼快就離開我們…什麼？什麼？你還在這裡，我的冒險家朋友？」

column1: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.
column2: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼。」

column1: Ach!  I was so busy I didn't notice you come in.  Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here.  Most people think this valley is cursed!
column2: 「哎呀！我太忙了，沒注意到你進來。歡迎，歡迎！我們這裡很少有新冒險者。大多數人都認為這個山谷被詛咒了！」

column1: It looks like this old Guild Master doesn't do too much adventuring any more. Still, he looks like he was plenty tough in his day, and he probably has many a tale to tell.
column2: 看起來這位年邁的公會主人不再進行太多冒險了。不過，他看起來在年輕時很強硬，他可能有很多故事要講。

column1: Adventurers
column2: 冒險者

column1: Antwerp
column2: 安特衛普

column1: Bakery
column2: 麵包店

column1: Bulletin Board
column2: 公告板

column1: Butcher
column2: 屠夫

column1: Centaur
column2: 半人馬

column1: Cheetaur
column2: 獵豹人

column1: Baron's Daughter
column2: 男爵之女

column1: Dragon
column2: 龍

column1: Forests
column2: 森林

column1: Dry Goods
column2: 雜貨

column1: Goon
column2: 惡棍

column1: Guild Hall
column2: 公會大廳

column1: Hans Halfwitten
column2: 漢斯·哈夫威騰

column1: Hero
column2: 英雄

column1: Magic Shop
column2: 魔法店

column1: Mantrays
column2: 曼崔斯

column1: Guild Master
column2: 公會主人

column1: Moose
column2: 駝鹿

column1: Mountains
column2: 山脈

column1: Ogres
column2: 食人妖

column1: Saurus Rex
column2: 索魯斯王

column1: Saurus
column2: 索魯斯

column1: Schultz Meistersson
column2: 舒爾茨·麥斯特松

column1: Baron's Son
column2: 男爵之子

column1: Tavern
column2: 酒館

column1: Tourists
column2: 觀光客

column1: Troll
column2: 巨魔

column1: Trophies
column2: 獎杯

column1: Waterfalls
column2: 瀑布

column1: The guildmaster appears to be hard of hearing.  Perhaps you should get closer.
column2: 公會主人似乎聽力不太好。也許你應該靠近一些。

column1: Ach, I was so busy I did not notice you come into the Adventurer's Guild Hall.  Welcome, welcome, it is so seldom we have new adventurers here.  Most people think this valley is cursed.
column2: 「哎呀，我太忙了，沒注意到你進入冒險者公會大廳。歡迎，歡迎，我們這裡很少有新冒險者。大多數人都認為這個山谷被詛咒了。」

column1: Hrrmph!  Ahem!  Yes, as I was saying...  Where were we?
column2: 「呃唔！咳！是的，我剛才說到…我們說到哪裡了？」

column1: Oh, what a glorious day it was!  I...  Oh, hello there.
column2: 「噢，那是多麼光榮的一天啊！我…噢，你好。」

column1: Then she turned around and I was staring into the biggest...  Oh, it's you, my friend.
column2: 「然後她轉過身來，我盯著最大的…噢，是你啊，我的朋友。」

column1: It was just yesterday that I drew my sword and started out...  Or was that the day before?  No, then last week...  Why, What was I talking about?
column2: 「那是昨天啊，我拔劍出發…或是前天？不，上週的事…為什麼，我在說什麼呢？」

column1: Try getting a little closer.
column2: 試著靠近一些。

column1: Did I ever tell you about the time when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a real Guild Hall. Now we are just old men and this is just a place to tell old stories.
column2: 「我有沒有告訴過你，舒爾茨和我清除這個山谷裡安特衛普的那段日子？是的，我們那時是真正的冒險者，這也是真正的公會大廳。現在我們只是老人，這只是個講老故事的地方。」

column1: This valley was overrun by those odd monsters one year.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to rid the valley of the things.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid.
column2: 「有一年，這個山谷被那些古怪的怪物佔領了。舒爾茨和我努力戰鬥來清除山谷中的那些東西。即使如此，我們可能已經失敗了，但多虧了那兩位奇特的觀光客來幫助我們。」

column1: She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogre. Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him.
column2: 「我認為她是我們問題的中心。芭芭雅嘎是一個強大的食人妖。史畢柏格男爵試圖強迫她離開這個山谷，但她詛咒了他。」

column1: Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero.
column2: 「現在男爵失去了一切，只剩下他的土地，我不知道他能保留多久。我們的山谷需要的是一位英雄。」

column1: I think the Bakery is almost always closed now.  The Baker tries to stay away from his wife.
column2: 「我認為麵包店現在幾乎總是關門的。麵包師傅試圖遠離他的妻子。」

column1: Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when I was younger.
column2: 「史畢柏格男爵曾經是我們山谷的勇敢保護者。我年輕時，我們常常一起冒險。」

column1: No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said he goes nowhere and sees no one.
column2: 「如果男爵沒有惹怒芭芭雅嘎，沒有山賊或怪物敢在這裡露面。現在據說他哪裡也不去，誰都不見。」

column1: Over on the wall over there is where the jobs are posted for adventurers. Some have been there a long time now.  There just aren't too many good adventurers around here, lately.
column2: 「那邊牆上就是發佈冒險家工作的地方。有些工作已經貼在那裡很久了。最近這裡就是沒有太多優秀的冒險者。」

column1: There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing the leaders of the brigands.  You should read the bulletin board for more information.
column2: 「任何能夠通過抓捕或殺死山賊頭目來制止山賊的人都會獲得獎勵。你應該看看公告板以獲得更多信息。」

column1: I don't think the Butcher Shop is open much any more, either.
column2: 「我認為屠夫店現在也很少開門了。」

column1: The castle is just north of the Healer's house.
column2: 「城堡就在治療師房子的北邊。」

column1: There are not too many Centaurs in this land.   Heinrich and Hilde are good people.
column2: 「這片土地上的半人馬不太多。海因里希和希爾德是好人。」

column1: Watch out for Cheetaurs!  I bare the scars of my fight with the one on the wall to this day.  Still, it is stuffed, and I am still alive.
column2: 「小心獵豹人！我至今還留著和牆上那一隻戰鬥的疤痕。不過，它是標本，我還活著。」

column1: Ja, with the Baron losing his son and daughter, Baba Yaga, all the monsters, and now the brigands, it has just been one thing after another.
column2: 「是啊，男爵失去了他的兒子和女兒、還有芭芭雅嘎、所有怪物，現在又有山賊，真是接二連三。」

column1: Elsa  was a beautiful eight year old child with blond hair and sky  blue eyes.  She was her father the Baron's joy.  Ten years ago, she was carried off by something which flew over the wall and away with her.
column2: 「艾爾莎是個美麗的八歲小孩，有著金色的頭髮和湛藍的眼睛。她是她父親男爵的喜悅。十年前，她被某種飛越城牆的東西帶走了。」

column1: The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up except the Baron's Jester, Yorick.
column2: 「尋找她持續了許多年，但最後除了男爵的弄臣尤瑞克外，所有人都放棄了。」

column1: One day years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them.
column2: 「多年前的某一天，一對龍試圖接管我們的山谷。我們冒險者騎馬出去迎戰他們。」

column1: I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse. He slew that dragon whose head you see on the wall, and the other one flew off.
column2: 「我仍然可以看到史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格騎著黑馬衝向前去。他殺死了你在牆上看到的那條龍，另一條飛走了。」

column1: I hear people see that other dragon flying high overhead sometimes, but it has never dared to attack us again.
column2: 「我聽說人們有時會看到那另一條龍在頭頂高飛，但它從未敢再攻擊我們。」

column1: The forests are full of monsters and strange creatures.
column2: 「森林裡滿是怪物和奇異的生物。」
